mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising policy rates, or if the sign of the relationship flips for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays low, real rates fall and the market interprets the stance as accommodative, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered cases do not show higher 20-day forward returns than non-triggered cases, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE identifies labor market deterioration that weakens earnings expectations and risk appetite, so higher unemployment predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return becomes non-negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Job growth in PAYEMS signals durable demand and operating leverage, so positive payroll momentum should lift S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum does not correspond to above-baseline 60-day forward S&P 500 returns, or if the predictive sign turns negative across any 4-quarter rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales growth in RSAFS reflects slowing consumer demand and revenue deterioration, so negative RSAFS momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if subzero RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns at a rate materially above 50%, or if the conditional return difference is not negative in rolling 2-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO indicates industrial output contraction and weaker cyclical earnings, so declining industrial production should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns negative after being positive
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contractions fail to predict below-average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign of the effect is positive in any sustained 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and RSAFS identifies broad demand destruction across housing and consumption, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in housing starts and retail sales do not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 5-day returns, or if the combined signal is not negative in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO together with stable or falling UNRATE indicates improving real activity without labor stress, supporting a positive S&P 500 response over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and non-rising UNRATE do not generate higher 60-day forward returns than other periods, or if the interaction becomes negative in any 3-year rolling sample.
